queries: (("Child, Preschool"[Mesh] OR "Infant"[Mesh] OR child*[tiab] OR infant*[tiab] OR toddler*[tiab] OR "under five"[tiab] OR "under-five"[tiab] OR "under 5"[tiab] OR "0-59 months"[tiab] OR "0 to 59 months"[tiab]) AND ("Developing Countries"[Mesh] OR LMIC*[tiab] OR "low- and middle-income countr*"[tiab] OR "low and middle income countr*"[tiab] OR "developing countr*"[tiab] OR "resource-limited"[tiab] OR "resource poor"[tiab] OR "sub-Saharan Africa"[tiab] OR "South Asia"[tiab] OR "Southeast Asia"[tiab] OR "Latin America"[tiab] OR "Caribbean"[tiab]) AND (stunt*[tiab] OR "height-for-age"[tiab] OR "length-for-age"[tiab] OR HAZ[tiab] OR LAZ[tiab] OR "linear growth"[tiab] OR "growth faltering"[tiab] OR "linear growth retard*"[tiab] OR "chronic malnutrition"[tiab] OR "chronic undernutrition"[tiab]))

(("Child, Preschool"[Mesh] OR "Infant"[Mesh] OR "under-five"[tiab] OR "0-59 months"[tiab]) AND ("Developing Countries"[Mesh] OR "developing countr*"[tiab] OR LMIC*[tiab] OR "low- and middle-income"[tiab]) AND (stunt*[tiab] OR "height-for-age"[tiab] OR "length-for-age"[tiab] OR HAZ[tiab] OR LAZ[tiab])) AND ("Demographic and Health Surveys"[Mesh] OR "Demographic and Health Survey"[tiab] OR "Demographic and Health Surveys"[tiab] OR DHS[tiab] OR "Multiple Indicator Cluster Surveys"[tiab] OR MICS[tiab] OR "national survey"[tiab] OR "population-based survey"[tiab] OR "household survey"[tiab])

(("Child, Preschool"[Mesh] OR "Infant"[Mesh]) AND ("Developing Countries"[Mesh] OR LMIC*[tiab] OR "developing countr*"[tiab])) AND (("stunted"[tiab] OR "stunting"[tiab]) AND ("non-stunted"[tiab] OR "not stunted"[tiab] OR "normal height-for-age"[tiab] OR "height-for-age within normal"[tiab] OR "HAZ >= -2"[tiab] OR "HAZ > -2"[tiab] OR "LAZ >= -2"[tiab]))

inclusion_criteria: Study design: Primary research using experimental, quasi-experimental, cohort, case-control, or cross-sectional designs.
Population: Human children aged 0–59 months residing in low- and middle-income countries (per World Bank classification at time of study).
Comparator: Includes a comparison group of non-stunted children (height-for-age z-score ≥ −2).
Outcomes: Reports stunting status defined by WHO Child Growth Standards (height-for-age z-score < −2), as prevalence, incidence, or risk estimates.
exclusion_criteria: Not in LMIC settings or does not provide separate data for children under 5 years.
Does not report stunting using WHO height-for-age z-score criteria or lacks a non-stunted comparator group.

Non-primary study designs (systematic reviews, meta-analyses, editorials, commentaries, letters, protocols), qualitative-only studies, case reports, or ecological studies without individual-level data.
Non-human studies or studies using only non-WHO growth references without WHO-defined stunting outcomes.
screening_criteria.description: Screening criteria to identify primary studies on children under 5 in LMICs that report WHO-defined stunting (HAZ < −2) with a non-stunted comparator, across experimental and observational designs.

included_articles.title: Predictors of Stunting and Pathway Analysis for Linear Growth among Children Aged Two to Three Years after a Trial of Small-Quantity Lipid-Based Nutrient Supplements and Home-Installed Growth Charts in Three Districts in Zambia.
A multilevel analysis of factors associated with stunting among children under five years in Lesotho: a study of the lesotho multiple cluster indicator survey 2018.

Is Infant and Young Child-feeding (IYCF) a potential double-duty strategy to prevent the double burden of malnutrition among children at the critical age? Evidence of association from urban slums in Pune, Maharashtra, India.
To work or not to work? Child stunting and maternal labour force participation in South Africa.

Exploring the determinants of undernutrition among children aged 6-59 months old in Malawi: Insights on religious affiliation, ethnicity, and nutritional status using the 2015-2016 Malawi demographic and health survey.
Coexisting predictors for undernutrition indices among under-five children in West Africa: application of a multilevel multivariate ordinal logistic regression model.
Analyzing childhood (0-59 months) malnutrition determinants in five West African countries of Gabon, Gambia, Liberia, Mauritania, and Nigeria using a generalized additive mixed model from DHS data.
Child undernutrition and its association with household environmental conditions in Bangladesh.
